channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Gerber recalls all batches of Soothe N Chew Teething Sticks nationwide
item.description: The recall follows consumer complaints, including one reported emergency room visit related to choking incidents, according to a release.
item.dc:creator: Reagan Wynn, NewsNation

item.title: Senate confirms fracking CEO Chris Wright to lead Energy Department
item.description: The Senate on Monday confirmed Chris Wright, the CEO of fracking company Liberty Energy, to lead the Energy Department. The vote was 59-38. Democrat Sens. Michael Bennet (Colo.), Ruben Gallego (Ariz.), Maggie Hassan (N.H.), Martin Heinrich (N.M.), John Hickenlooper (Colo.), Ben Ray Luján (N.M.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, NewsNation

item.title: Duffy tells DOT to prioritize areas with high birth rates
item.description: Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy in a memo told the Department of Transportation (DOT) to prioritize communities that have higher birth and marriage rates.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: ACLU sues University of Michigan over campus bans for pro-Palestinian protesters
item.description: LANSING, Mich. (AP) — Jonathan Zou, a second-year student at the University of Michigan, was among the thousands of students who joined pro-Palestinian protests on university campuses nationwide this past year.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Gerber recalls all batches of Soothe N Chew Teething Sticks nationwide
item.description: Gerber Products Company has announced a nationwide recall and discontinuation of all batches of its Soothe N Chew Teething Sticks due to a potential choking hazard for infants and young children.
item.dc:creator: Reagan Wynn, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Salvage crews recover engine, large portion of jet from river after deadly air collision near DC
item.description: ARLINGTON, Va. (AP) — Salvage crews have recovered an engine and large pieces of fuselage and are working to retrieve a wing from the wreckage of a commercial airliner involved in last week's midair collision near Washington’s Reagan National Airport, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: DAVID R. MARTIN, MICHAEL R. SISAK and CLAUDIA LAUER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Tariff threats take aim at fentanyl trafficking. Here's how the drug reaches the US
item.description: President Donald Trump's plan to impose tariffs on goods from Mexico, Canada and China is partly aimed at combating the illicit flow of fentanyl into the U.S., where the opioid is blamed for some 70,000 overdose deaths annually. Mexico agreed Monday to send 10,000 troops to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Association representing thousands of FBI agents appeals to Congress to protect their jobs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The association representing thousands of FBI agents urged congressional leaders Monday to protect the jobs of employees at risk of punishment or possibly termination over their participation in investigations into the Jan. 6, 2021, riot at the U.S. Capitol.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Transportation secretary says 'expansive' impact zone was left by Philadelphia medical plane crash
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — The impact area where a medical transportation plane crashed in Philadelphia was notably “expansive,” U.S. Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy said Monday after viewing the site with investigators and city officials. Duffy said he was struck by “how expansive it was.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Trump administration hires hardcore pro-Trump ideologue to run public diplomacy at State Department
item.description: SAN SALVADOR (AP) — The Trump administration has hired a “Make America Great Again” ideologue to run the State Department’s worldwide public diplomacy efforts, according to the man put forward for the post and three current department officials.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Texas National Guard can now arrest and detain people who illegally enter the US
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Texas National Guard soldiers can arrest and detain people for entering the U.S. illegally from Mexico under an agreement with the Trump administration that expands the military's role in immigration enforcement.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump administration ends temporary deportation protection for 350,000 Venezuelans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's administration is ending protections that shielded roughly 350,000 Venezuelans from deportation, leaving them with two months before they lose their right to work in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA and GISELA SALOMON, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Trump administration opens antisemitism inquiries at 5 colleges including Columbia and Berkeley
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration is opening new investigations into allegations of antisemitism at five U.S. universities including Columbia and the University of California, Berkeley, the Education Department announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Indianapolis man used fake deeds to sell 5 homes he didn't own
item.description: In addition to his 10-year federal prison sentence, James Henley, 35, was ordered to pay nearly $2 million in restitution.
item.dc:creator: Matt Christy, NewsNation

item.title: Musk adds Shell, Nestlé, Lego to X advertiser 'boycott' lawsuit
item.description: X is adding several more companies to its lawsuit accusing firms of conspiring on an advertising "boycott" on the social media platform.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pacific Coast Highway reopens nearly a month after devastating Southern California wildfires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Pacific Coast Highway reopened Monday, nearly a month after it was closed when flames from the deadly Palisades Fire ripped through hillside Los Angeles neighborhoods, destroying properties all the way down to the beach.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 55 victims in Washington, DC midair collision pulled from Potomac River as recovery operation continues
item.description: The bodies of 55 victims have been pulled from the Potomac River in Washington, D.C., following the midair collision between a commercial jet and a Black Hark helicopter on Jan. 29.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu heads to US to discuss 'victory over Hamas' with Trump
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Sunday he will discuss "victory over Hamas,” countering Iran and expanding diplomatic relations with Arab countries in his meeting with President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: EU warns Trump it would ‘respond firmly’ to tariffs
item.description: “The EU would respond firmly to any trading partner that unfairly or arbitrarily imposes tariffs on EU goods,” a spokesperson said. “There is a lot at stake.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Jimmy Carter wins posthumous Grammy for narrating an audiobook of his Sunday school lessons
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Former President Jimmy Carter has won a posthumous Grammy award. Carter, the peanut farmer who won the presidency in the wake of the Watergate scandal and Vietnam War, died in December at age 100.
item.dc:creator: MARIA SHERMAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Officials say remains of 55 of 67 victims of midair collision have been recovered and identified
item.description: ARLINGTON, Va. (AP) — Families of victims of the deadliest U.S. air disaster since 2001 visited the crash site on Sunday and divers scoured the submerged wreckage for more remains after authorities said they've recovered and identified 55 of the 67 people killed. Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: DAVID R. MARTIN, MICHAEL R. SISAK and CLAUDIA LAUER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Remains of North Carolina mom missing since 2023 found by dive team: deputies
item.description: Dana Mustian never showed up at her daughter’s 6th birthday party in December 2023. Her remains have since been located by a dive team.
item.dc:creator: Rodney Overton, ABC 4

item.title: USAID security leaders on leave after trying to keep Musk's DOGE from classified info: officials
item.description: The two USAID security officials believed themselves legally obligated to deny access to Musk's DOGE crew since it lacked high-enough security clearance to access the information.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Eerie photos show interior of doomed medevac jet that crashed in Philadelphia
item.description: New photos provided by Jet Rescue Air shows the interior of a medevac jet that crashed in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania on Friday, killing at least seven people and injuring 19 others.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Families visit DC crash site days after the deadliest US air disaster in a generation
item.description: Dozens of people walked along the banks of the Potomac River near Reagan National Airport, close to where an American Airlines jet and an Army Black Hawk helicopter collided Wednesday, killing all 67 aboard.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Grammy Awards: How to watch the ceremony, red carpet arrivals, interviews
item.description: The 67th annual Grammy Awards are set to recognize some of the most talented performers and biggest hits in pop music on Sunday, with different ways to watch the festivities.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Canadian fans boo U.S. National Anthem at hockey game amid trade war
item.description: Canadians aren’t too happy with the tariffs being imposed on their country by U.S. President Donald Trump. Trump signed an order Saturday to impose stiff tariffs on imports from Canada as well as Mexico and China, which drew swift retaliation and sparked a trade war.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: How to save money on your taxes
item.description: You may qualify for tax credits for caring for a child or dependent or investing in clean energy for your home.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, KRON4

item.title: Will La Niña be a 'bust' this winter? Meteorologist explains why it may feel like it
item.description: After months of teasing, a La Niña winter has finally started – but we don't blame you if you haven't noticed.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KRON4

item.title: A 2,500th 3-pointer, a triple-double, Bronny's basket give LeBron James another memorable MSG game
item.description: A 2,500th 3-pointer for his career and his 10th triple-double this season. Not a bad 31st trip to Madison Square Garden for LeBron James. Pretty good first one for Bronny James, too.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MAHONEY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Straka has late surge to regain Pebble Beach lead over Rory McIlroy and Shane Lowry
item.description: Sepp Straka watched his shot on the 10th sail over the cliff and onto the beach, starting a stretch of three straight bogeys. He rallied with four birdies over the final five holes to salvage a 2-under 70 and a one-shot lead over McIlroy and Shane Lowry.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump follows through on long-standing pledge, signs tariffs on Canada, Mexico and China
item.description: "Tariffs on imports from Canada, Mexico, and China are SIGNED!" White House spokesperson Harrison Fields posted on the social media platform X.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Seven dead, 19 injured in 'high-impact' air ambulance crash in Philadelphia
item.description: A person in a car was the seventh fatal victim of the fiery crash of an air ambulance onto a busy Philadelphia street, authorities said Saturday, as investigators sifted through burned cars, damaged homes and charred debris for clues to determine why the aircraft plummeted shortly after takeoff.
item.dc:creator: MATT ROURKE and MARC LEVY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: With plans in the works and troops on the way, military assumes a mostly familiar role on the border
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — Dozens of Marines unfurled coils of concertina wire — one on the ground and one slightly above — making it more difficult to climb a border wall separating Tijuana from San Diego.
item.dc:creator: ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Data from the deadliest U.S. air accident in a generation show conflicting altitude readings
item.description: Recovery crews and divers searched the Potomac River for remains and cleared wreckage Saturday from the midair collision of a passenger jet and Army helicopter that killed 67 people.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Author's story of coping after a wildfire resonates with community affected by latest LA-area fires
item.description: PASADENA, Calif. (AP) — On a quiet summer evening in June 1990, Pico Iyer sat in his family home in Santa Barbara, California, when suddenly, he was surrounded by walls of flames five stories high.
item.dc:creator: DEEPA BHARATH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 40+ bodies pulled from Potomac near Reagan National
item.description: More than 40 bodies have been recovered from the Potomac River after a deadly mid-air collision sent an Army Black Hawk helicopter and American Airlines plane diving into the icy water earlier this week, according to The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC
